Here are my findings:\n\n### Bug Analysis\nThe bug report indicates that Intel Wireless-AC 9260 (PCI device ID 0x2526) is missing subdevice entries, preventing proper firmware loading in Linux kernel 5.6-rc2.\n\n### Root Cause Identified\n**File**: `drivers/net/wireless/intel/iwlwifi/pcie/drv.c`\n\n**Buggy Data Structure**: `iwl_dev_info_table` (defined at line 978)\n\n**Specific Issue**: \n- The `iwl_dev_info_table` is missing entries for PCI subdevices 0x401D, 0x4018, and 0x401C for device 0x2526\n- While the `iwl_hw_card_ids` table has a catch-all entry (line 599: `{IWL_PCI_DEVICE(0x2526, PCI_ANY_ID, iwl9000_trans_cfg)}`) that allows the driver to be loaded, the missing entries in `iwl_dev_info_table` prevent proper device-specific configuration\n- During PCI probe (in `iwl_pci_probe` function starting at line 1002), the driver searches `iwl_dev_info_table` to find matching device configuration - without these entries, the device falls back to default configuration and cannot load the correct firmware\n\n**Confidence**: 0.85\n\nThe results have been written to `/workspace/fault_localization_result.json`."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":8,"cache_creation_input_tokens":823,"cache_read_input_tokens":46950,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":823},"output_tokens":4,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"51ac3e13-f19d-4a05-80ae-014860ca9c62","uuid":"de9f8302-62c2-47d9-acdb-841f6cd588b6"}
